channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LIVE: Cities across the globe prepare to welcome the New Year in celebrations tinged with local traditions
item.description: Cities around the world are readying to ring in the New Year with celebrations highlighting local cultures and traditions, after a year roiled by ongoing conflict and political instability.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE STREAM: New Year’s Eve Ball Drop in Times Square NYC
item.description: Watch live streaming coverage of the annual Times Square Ball Drop in New York City on New Year’s Eve!
item.dc:creator: Ken Haddad, Local 4

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Georgia AG urges state Supreme Court to reject DA Willis’ appeal in Trump case
item.description: The Georgia attorney general urged the state Supreme Court to not consider an appeal from Fulton County DA Fani Willis, who was removed from the Trump election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police investigating after hiker finds badly decomposed body hundred of yards down California cliffside
item.description: Homicide detectives are investigating after a body was found hundreds of yards off a California cliff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat and a pet food recall are raising questions about the ongoing outbreak of bird flu and how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to watch WWE's 'Raw' on Netflix: What to know before the Jan. 6 debut
item.description: After 21 years, WWE's "RAW" is leaving cable to join Netflix every Monday night beginning Jan. 6, meaning streaming is the only way to watch the longest-running weekly episodic show.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KRON4

item.title: Dunkin’ to debut Sabrina Carpenter-inspired espresso drink
item.description: Sabrina Carpenter and Dunkin have been working late throughout the holiday season. On Monday, the breakfast chain announced that Sabrina’s Brown Sugar Shakin’ Espresso, a nod to the Grammy-nominated artist’s chart-topping hit, “Espresso,” will be coming to menus on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Lost hunters located by thermal drones in Tennessee
item.description: A hunter called emergency services just after 9:30 p.m. on Friday, Dec. 27 to report that they were lost.
item.dc:creator: Aubriella Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Elk with chair on head in Ruidoso is able to move, eat
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- An elk with a chair on its head is being reported in the Ruidoso area, but authorities say the animal has been evaluated and is doing OK. The Ruidoso Police Department said Saturday evening, Dec.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Ford Museum will open display honoring Jimmy Carter's life
item.description: After the death of former President Jimmy Carter, the Gerald R. Ford Museum in Grand Rapids is opening a new display to commemorate his life.
item.dc:creator: Anna Skog, NewsNation

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, KRON4

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: LeBron James at 40: A milestone birthday arrives Monday for the NBA's all-time scoring leader
item.description: When LeBron James broke another NBA record earlier this month, the one for most regular-season minutes played in a career, his Los Angeles Lakers teammates handled the moment in typical locker room fashion. They made fun of him. “They told me I’m old as hell,” James quipped.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UPDATE: Search for suspects continues in Mississippi after Ford pickup found
item.description: SATURDAY 12/28/2024 10:45 a.m. (EL PASO COUNTY, Colo.) — EPSO reported that law enforcement in Hattiesburg, Mississippi located the 1994 Ford F-150 pick-up truck early on Saturday, Dec. 28.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Eberhardt, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Convicted Conway bank killer asks for compassionate release
item.description: Council was convicted in September 2019 in the 2017 double murders of Crescom Bank employees Donna Major and Katie Skeen.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 men rescued after boat runs out of gas, capsizes near Desecheo Island
item.description: SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO (WFLA)— Two men were rescued on Saturday by the Coast Guard after their boat ran out of fuel and capsized south of Desecheo Island. Coast Guard Sector San Juan watchstanders got an unregistered digital selective calling alert around 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning actor who starred in the sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning Broadway actor who starred in TV sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died. She was 87.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Significant milestones in life and career of Jimmy Carter
item.description: — Oct. 1, 1924: James Earl Carter Jr. is born in Plains, Georgia, son of James Sr. and Lillian Gordy Carter. — June 1946: Carter graduates from the U.S. Naval Academy. — July 1946: Carter marries Rosalynn Smith, in Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supplemental Security Income payment schedule is wonky to start 2025: What to know
item.description: Regardless of when your payment comes, know that you will still receive all of your intended checks in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: 2 dead after search for Sasquatch in Washington national forest
item.description: Two men searching for Sasquatch, also known as Bigfoot, over Christmas week were found dead in the forest in the state of Washington after not coming home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, ABC 4

item.title: More battles over transgender rights await Supreme Court
item.description: Cases implicating transgender rights are piling up at the Supreme Court as it begins drafting its opinion on whether states can ban gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers' Ohtani and wife will soon be welcoming a baby to the family
item.description: Between a marriage announcement, third MVP award and World Series title, Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani had a pretty good 2024. And now, it looks like 2025 may be even better for the Japanese two-way baseball phenom.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: ABC 4

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
